task.title: Creating a model workspace for push-to-client configuration

titlealts.navtitle: Creating a model workspace for push-to-client configuration
titlealts.searchtitle: Creating a model workspace for push-to-client configuration

task.shortdesc: A model workspace is a workspace from which you create the remote system connections, configuration files, and preference settings to be distributed to other client workstations.

taskbody.context: Creating a model workspace for push-to-client configurations is no different from creating any other workspace. It is good practice to dedicate a workspace to configuration so that other development activities do not alter the remote system connections, configuration files, and preference settings that are intended for general distribution. The purpose of using a model workspace is for the workspace owner (or push-to-client administrator) to set up preferences, system connections, and configurations the way ordinary users must have them in their workspaces. Set up the workspace by using the product user interface and then export those settings by using the configuration export wizard.

step.cmd: For information about creating a workspace, see Switching workspaces.
For information about group-level control of configurations, see in the Host Configuration Reference.
